title: Bridge Paper: Is neuroimaging ready for the classroom? A systematic review of hyperscanning studies in learning.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Social Incarnation** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Intersubjectivity — second-person neuroscience, embodied empathy, shared intentionality, social predictive processing.
**Phase context:** intersubjectivity, embodied empathy, predictive processing social cognition, shared intentionality, second-person neuroscience

Paper: Is neuroimaging ready for the classroom? A systematic review of hyperscanning studies in learning.
DOI: 10.1016/j.neuroimage.2023.120367
Abstract: Whether education research can be informed by findings from neuroscience studies has been hotly debated since Bruer's (1997) famous claim that neuroscience and education are "a bridge too far". However, this claim came before recent advancements in portable electroencephalography (EEG) and functional near-infrared spectroscopy (fNIRS) technologies, and second-person neuroscience techniques that brought about significant headway in understanding instructor-learner interactions in the classroom.
